docs.title: Home
docs.text:
This is the Portfolio for Research Data Management Services (1) in the Energy Domain. The goal of this portfolio is to show and navigate to relevant services - in the future it might look like this.

  1. Check out the information on Research Data Management from the German Research Foundation - DFG.

For which step of your research process do you need a service?

flowchart LR
  id1("1. Planning 📗") --> id2("2. Production 🔧")
  id2 --> id3("3. Analysis ⚙️")
  id3 --> id4("4. Storage 💾")
  id4 --> id5("5. Access 💻")
  id5 --> id6("6. Reuse 🔁")
  id6 --> id1
  id7("Services for developers")
  click id1 "categories/#dlcplanning" _blank
  click id2 "categories/#dlcproduction" _blank
  click id3 "categories/#dlcanalysis" _blank
  click id4 "categories/#dlcstorage" _blank

  click id6 "categories/#dlcre-use" _blank
  click id7 "categories/#dev" _blank

docs.title: What is a service?
docs.text:
Closely alligned with the Base4NFDI defintion, we define a service as follows:

Definition

A service is a technical-oganisational solution that typically offers

  • ☁️ storage and computing services
  • 🔁 software, processes and workflows
  • ☎️ support for service desk

... with different levels of technicity.

docs.title: What are the Requirements for becoming an NFDI4Energy Service?
docs.text:
This site collects data services that are relevant in the energy domain. To appear in this Collection, the following criteria are used:

  • The Service targets the german energy sector.
  • The Service targets one or multiple steps in the Data Life Cycle or helps to build services for the Data Life Cycle.
  • The Service already complies with the FAIR criteria or it is aiming to do so in the future.
  • The Service is running and available online.

Services that aim to become part of the NFDI4Energy Service Portfolio but do not yet meet all requirements are collected as candidates.

You think a requirement is missing here?

Discuss requirements with us, either via mail service[@]nfdi4energy.org or in the github issue.

docs.title: Categorization based on the Data Life Cycle
docs.text:
Data Services are tools that help researchers in one or multiple steps of the Research Data Management Life Cycle. Hence, services can get one or multiple of the tags planning, production, analysis, storage, access, and re-use. Additionally, Services for developers can help developers to build services.

Research Data Management Life Cycle.

docs.title: Categorization based on the DFG Definitions
docs.text:
These categories are relevant for the DFG reporting. They can be accessed from the nfdi1000 Excel sheet here and the Guide

First, Categories are defined based on the de.NBI definitions (see Drop Down Categories from the reporting excel sheet, Tab 5)

Category Definition Database software providing large amounts of structured data to the user. Usually the data can be uploaded, accessed, searched and/or downloaded via a web browser. Library / API collection of pre‐implemented functions for a specific task that can be accessed via a well‐ defined interfaces. Workflow software that combines multiple tools / applications. They may be used locally or remotely via the internet. Tools / applications software that can be downloaded and executed locally on the users' hardware. Web applications software that is installed on a server and can be used by users via a web page and the internet. Storage Not defined by de.NBI Data Curation Not defined by de.NBI

de.NBI additionally defines Support / consulting as "service with direct user contact for topics going beyond the support for the other services".

docs.title: Categorization based on the DFG Service Descriptions
docs.text:
The DFG Reporting asks for the state of the software. We map these states to TLR levels, where we follow the Criteria for Basic Services from base4nfdi.

DFG Service Description Technology Readiness Level TLR Development 1-4 Prototype 5-6 Operation 7-9 Terminated

Is the software open-source? If yes, add the tag

  • open-source

docs.title: NFDI4Energy Services
docs.text:
This is a work in progress.

The list on the left site might change over time. Click any of the services to get more infomation. Candidates are Services which might be part of the official Service Portfolio in the future.

docs.title: Competence Service
docs.text: A service to help interested parties to identify clusters of competence and enable connections between them.

docs.title: Institution
docs.text: OFFIS - Standardized Systems Engineering and Assessment

docs.title: Connection to other NFDI4Energy Services
docs.text: • Identity & Access Managenment

docs.title: What is missing to become an NFDI4Energy Service?
docs.text: • Needs to be hosted somewhere.

docs.title: Link
docs.text: Not hosted yet.

docs.title: DaceDS
docs.text: daceDS is a data-centric Distributed Simulation framework that focuses on data and loose couplings between components.

docs.title: Leibniz Data Manager
docs.text: The Leibniz Data Manager is a CKAN distribution tailored for Research Data Managment and was developed to support the aspect of better re-usability of research data.

docs.title: Link
docs.text: https://projects.tib.eu/datamanager/

docs.title: mosaik
docs.text: Mosaik allows you to reuse and combine existing simulation models and simulators to create large-scale Smart Grid scenarios – and by large-scale we mean thousands of simulated entities distributed over multiple simulator processes. These scenarios can then serve as test bed for various types of control strategies (e.g., multi-agent systems (MAS) or centralized control).

docs.title: Link
docs.text:
• https://mosaik.offis.de
  • Documentation Page

docs.title: Open Energy Academy
docs.text: A collection of training courses, tutorials, and questions from the Open Energy Platform.

docs.title: Link
docs.text:
• https://openenergyplatform.github.io/academy/
  • https://openenergyplatform.org/

docs.title: Open Energy Database
docs.text: The Open Energy Database is an open community database for energy, climate and modelling data. Everybody can contribute to the database by adding, editing, and reviewing information.

docs.title: Components
docs.text:
• Open Metadata Builder
  • Review Process
  • OE Dialect
  • OEP Client
  • Data Table Wizard
  • Ontology Viewer

docs.title: Link
docs.text:
• https://openenergyplatform.org/dataedit/schemas
  • https://openenergyplatform.org/

docs.title: Open Energy Databus
docs.text: The Open Energy Databus is a transformative platform for agile data integration, collaboration, and automation via a structured metadata Knowledge Graph.

docs.title: Link
docs.text: https://databus.openenergyplatform.org/

docs.title: Open Energy Factsheets
docs.text: The Factsheets are a standardized collection and presentation of information about modelling frameworks and models used in climate and energy system modelling.

docs.title: Link
docs.text:
• https://openenergyplatform.org/factsheets/models/
  • https://openenergyplatform.org/factsheets/frameworks/

docs.title: Open Research Knowledge Graph
docs.text: The Open Research Knowledge Graph (ORKG) aims to describe research papers in a structured manner. With the ORKG, papers are easier to find and compare.

docs.title: Link
docs.text:
• https://orkg.org/
  • Overview of the ORKG

docs.title: TIB Terminology Service
docs.text: The TIB Terminology Service provides a single point of access to terminologies from domains such as architecture, chemistry, computer science, mathematics and physics. You can browse ontologies through the website or use its API to retrieve terminological information and use it in your technical services.

docs.title: Link
docs.text: https://terminology.tib.eu/ts

docs.title: VILLAS
docs.text: VILLAS is a framework for coupling test beds and real-time simulators between geographically distributed laboratories.

docs.title: Link
docs.text:
• https://villas.fein-aachen.org/
  • Documentation Page
